heatindexbr 0.2.0

    • Methodology: why trust the raster?
    • Primeiros passos com o heatindexbr

Extract Heat Index statistics for a custom spatial area

Source: R/extract_shape.R
hi_shape.Rd

Extracts Heat Index statistics from the climatology raster for any user-supplied sf polygon. The full 288-band stack is downloaded and cached on first use; subsequent calls read from the local cache.

Usage

hi_shape(
  shape,
  month = NULL,
  hour_local = NULL,
  product = c("climatology", "synoptic_2025"),
  stat = "mean",
  return_raster = TRUE,
  cache_dir = NULL
)

Arguments

shape

An sf object or a path to a shapefile.

month

Integer (1-12). Month of interest. If NULL, all 12 months are returned.

hour_local

Integer (0-23) or character such as "15h". Local hour (UTC-3). If NULL, all 24 hours are returned.

product

Character. "climatology" (default) or "synoptic_2025".

stat

Character. Statistic to compute over pixels. One of "mean" (default), "median", "min", or "max". If exactextractr is installed, it is used for more accurate polygon extraction.

return_raster

Logical. If TRUE, also returns the masked SpatRaster cropped to shape. Default is TRUE.

cache_dir

Character. Cache directory passed to hi_download().

Value

A named list with:

stats

A data frame with extraction results per month and hour.

raster

A SpatRaster masked to shape (only if return_raster = TRUE).

Examples

# \donttest{
# area <- sf::st_read("my_area.shp")
# res  <- hi_shape(area, month = 1, hour_local = 15)
# res$stats
# }
